data.body:
DESIGN SESSION — P1.4 ticket-store gc / bounded-growth (2026-06-18)

OUTCOME: superseded epic seek-algae-reign (a240-d692-a86f-411c, now CLOSED) with a
new peer-aligned epic minor-haste-ogle (97e7-cf42-5c00-462c). Closed orthogonal
child 4253-ced1-6c1d-47f4 (hard-delete story; reopenable). Filed 2 follow-ups:
sleek-bur-train (e3d0-16d9-61ca-4862, write-time op batching) and long-lath-fairy
(0241-c6c1-0a20-491b, move .bridge_state off the shared branch), both
discovered_from the new epic.

PROBLEM. The old epic proposed a bespoke operator `rebar gc` with hand-tuned
reflog/prune windows (default 14d) to protect a reflog-only recovery net. The
write path forces gc.auto=0 (event_append.py:74-84 + compact.py:61, txn.py:217/413,
init.py:182) solely to keep the reflog alive, because sync.py _do_reconverge does
`git reset --hard origin/tickets` in the unrelated-histories path (~71-85), which
orphans local commits recoverable only via reflog (warning at sync.py:81). No
durable ref is saved before the reset.

EXPERIMENTS (observed git execution, git 2.54; tmp repos):
- EXP-A: reachable branch commits survive `git gc --prune=now` -> un-pushed-on-branch work is prune-immune.
- EXP-B: a reset-orphaned commit survives `--prune=now` until `reflog expire`; the reflog (not prune) is the protection.
- EXP-C: fresh reflog entries protect; prune is object-mtime gated (packing can confound loose-only `git prune`).
- EXP-D: `git repack --cruft -d` packs unreachable into one pack w/ .mtimes, orphans preserved.
- EXP-E: 200 append-only commits = 600 objects = 2.5M; `git gc` -> 104K with all 600 objects intact => ~96% reclaim is repack/delta, ZERO prune, ZERO recovery risk.
- EXP-F: the old epic's exact recipe yields a cruft pack BY DEFAULT (gc.cruftPacks default-on since git 2.41).
- EXP-G: on an append-only branch, compacted/"deleted" event blobs stay reachable from prior history; even `gc --prune=now` cannot reclaim them => `--compact-first` reclaims nothing for gc.
- EXP-H: a durable ref makes an orphaned commit survive a FULL reflog wipe + `gc --prune=now`; deleting the ref gives controlled disposal. Reachability, not the reflog, is the durable guarantee.

WEB RESEARCH — PRIOR ART (verifiable):
- git-bug: gc.auto ON, no gc command, DAG union merge (ff-only, both parents), never reset --hard; operationPack batches ops into one blob/commit.
  https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/entity_actions.go
  https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/operation_pack.go
- git-appraise: git-notes unioned via cat_sort_uniq; archives old head to a ref before its one rewrite "to keep history from being garbage collected".
  https://github.com/google/git-appraise/blob/master/repository/git.go
  https://github.com/google/git-appraise/blob/master/review/review.go
- git-issue: one-commit-per-change, stock gc, no bespoke maintenance. https://github.com/dspinellis/git-issue
- jujutsu (jj): op-log recovery materialized as refs/jj/keep/; `jj util gc` safe because it only collects objects unreachable from retained refs (gc-reachability == recovery-guarantee co-design).
  https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/operation-log/  https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/technical/architecture/
- git: gc.auto default (6700) is normal; cruft packs default 2.37/2.41; `--prune=now` risks corruption under concurrent writers; `git maintenance` is the scale-up scheduler; disabling gc.auto WITHOUT out-of-band maintenance is the anti-pattern (Scalar/GitLab).
  https://git-scm.com/docs/git-gc  https://git-scm.com/docs/git-maintenance
  https://github.blog/open-source/git/highlights-from-git-2-37/  https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/introducing-scalar/

KEY FINDINGS:
1. On an append-only branch gc's value is repack/delta (safe), not prune. The old epic concentrated all design+risk on the prune/reflog path, which reclaims ~nothing here; `--compact-first` reclaims nothing (EXP-G).
2. The 14d reflog window shrank BOTH reachable (git default 90d) and unreachable (30d) reflog -> needlessly shrinks the recovery net 6x; reachable objects are prune-immune anyway (EXP-A).
3. The mid-recovery guard (check_no_rebase_in_progress) cannot see `reset --hard` (atomic, no marker).
4. No comparable tool builds a bespoke gc / reflog windows / recover refs. They trust stock gc and recover by union, never discarding (git-appraise even archives to a REF, not the reflog).

DESIGN ITERATION. v1 proposal (durable recover refs around the reset + retention + `rebar recover`) was rejected on the principle "if peers don't build it, ask why": peers don't need recover refs because they never discard. v2 (final): remove the discard itself.

FINAL PLAN (epic minor-haste-ogle). INVARIANT: after union recovery every commit
rebar cares about is ref-reachable, so stock `git gc` is safe by construction (jj's
co-design lesson, free). WU-1 re-enable gc.auto (remove _ensure_gc_auto_zero + the
four gc.auto 0 writes; gc.autoDetach=true at init; idempotent `--unset gc.auto`
migration). WU-2 replace sync.py Path-1 `reset --hard` with
`git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` REUSING the existing Path-3 conflict
handling. WU-3 `.gitattributes merge=ours` for `.bridge_state/*` + `.reconciler-*`
(merge=union is WRONG for JSON; push.py already handles these conflicts). WU-4 docs
(concurrency.md:165-196 + sync.py docstring) + state the invariant + note `git
maintenance` as scale-up.

TWO OPUS REVIEWS:
- Principal engineer: VERDICT ship-with-changes. Corrected scope (the bespoke
  machinery lives only in docs, not src; real delta is small), flagged the true
  union conflict surface (.bridge_state/bindings.json + reconciler lock/gate files,
  not just .gitignore), confirmed union is ALREADY proven in-repo (Path 3 + push.py),
  confirmed init already adopts origin/tickets so Path 1 is the narrow offline-init
  residual, confirmed no test asserts gc.auto==0 or reflog recovery (low blast
  radius), required doc updates. Simplest-safe variant: gitignore the mutable root
  files off the branch (=> follow-up long-lath-fairy).
- Peer comparison: VERDICT partial-to-strong, fully peer-aligned once the invariant
  is stated and `git maintenance` is noted. Confirmed every git-native peer leaves
  gc.auto on / ships no gc / unions-or-never-discards. Surfaced jj co-design (the
  invariant) and git-bug write-time batching (=> follow-up sleek-bur-train).
